title: Activity 9.6 — Separation Methods Sorter

cards.text: Taking out green chillies from cooked dalia or poha
Sorting sawdust and iron nails from building material
Picking marigold flowers from a heap to make a garland
Separating coconut pieces from rice flour

remove.label: Remove unwanted component
remove.description: One component is unwanted and should be removed

separate.label: Separate two useful components
separate.description: Both components are useful and need to be separated

schema.description: Interactive 5-step drag-and-drop activity for NCERT Grade 6 Science Chapter 9 (Methods of Separation in Everyday Life). Students sort 10 real-life scenarios into two baskets based on the purpose of separation.

curriculum.subject: Science
curriculum.chapterTitle: Methods of Separation in Everyday Life

steps.name: Introduction
steps.description: Shows all 10 separation scenario cards and the two basket types with clear labels

steps.name: Prediction
steps.description: Students tap each card to register that they've considered its placement before sorting

steps.name: Sort Cards 1–5
steps.description: Drag-and-drop or tap-to-select first 5 cards into baskets. Instant correct/wrong feedback per card.

steps.name: Sort Cards 6–10
steps.description: Drag-and-drop or tap-to-select second 5 cards. Same feedback mechanism.

steps.name: Summary & Score
steps.description: Final score out of 10, method revealed per card, key conceptual insight shown

showStepIndicator.description: Show step dot indicator in header

cards.description: Override or extend the 10 scenario cards

agent_examples.title: Default full activity
agent_examples.description: Launch the full 5-step activity with all defaults

agent_examples.title: Dark mode version
agent_examples.description: Same tool in dark mode for night use

agent_examples.title: Jump to sorting step
agent_examples.description: Skip introduction, start directly at first sort

instructions_for_student: In Activity 9.6, you need to decide WHY we separate substances — is it to remove something unwanted, or to get two useful things apart? Drag each card into the correct basket and find out your score at the end!
learning_outcomes: Distinguish between the two purposes of separation: removing unwanted components vs. separating two useful components
Apply this understanding to 10 real-life scenarios from the NCERT textbook
Reinforce knowledge of separation methods: handpicking, churning, magnetic separation, sieving, decantation, evaporation
